vision_report.compositional_truth_check: The registry is largely inconsistent with the visual evidence. P1 (String of Nickels) is not visible as a plant, only a pot. P2/P4 are not clearly identifiable as distinct specimens. P3 (Pothos) is present but appears to be a soil-based substrate with a white rabbit anchor.

inventory_reconciliation.P1: Systemic Loss: No foliage detected in yellow pot.
inventory_reconciliation.P2: Systemic Loss: No distinct Mexican Mint foliage detected.
inventory_reconciliation.P3: Present: Pothos/Soil substrate with white rabbit anchor.
inventory_reconciliation.P4: Systemic Loss: No distinct Silver Guest foliage detected.

plant_audit.P1: Empty yellow pot; soil surface appears undisturbed.
plant_audit.P2: Empty black pot; no vegetative growth.
plant_audit.P3: Contains white rabbit anchor; soil appears dark and potentially over-saturated.

biome_observations.soil_texture: Consistently dark and damp across all pots.
biome_observations.debris: Introduction of a metallic spoon (T-1) and a cylindrical object/tube (CURRENT) near the yellow pot.

temporal_deltas.T-3_to_T-2: Minimal change; lighting conditions shifted to lower visibility.
temporal_deltas.T-2_to_T-1: Introduction of a metal spoon into the yellow pot area.
temporal_deltas.T-1_to_CURRENT: Removal of the spoon; introduction of a cylindrical object (likely a tube or applicator) adjacent to the yellow pot.

vision_report.visual_health_inference: The biome is currently in a state of 'Stasis/Neglect'. The lack of visible foliage in P1, P2, and P4 suggests either total loss or dormancy. P3 is the only site with a structural anchor, but no active growth is visible.
anomalies: Absence of expected foliage in P1, P2, P4
vision_report.narrative_description: The audit reveals a desk environment where the botanical registry is not currently supported by visible plant life. The pots appear to be empty of active foliage. Recent interventions include the placement of a spoon and a cylindrical object, suggesting human activity or maintenance, though the plants themselves remain absent or dormant.
